title: Talra Wildlife Sanctuary
tagline: Talra Wildlife Sanctuary — wildlife reserve of Himachal Pradesh

overview.short: Talra Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Shimla, Himachal Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Talra Wildlife Sanctuary is located in Shimla, Himachal Pradesh, India. It became a wildlife sanctuary in 1962. This wildlife sanctuary covers an area of 40 km2. It is an eco-sensitive zone, notified by the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MOEFCC).

topPlaces.name: Minda (village)
topPlaces.description: Minda (or Mindha)is a village located in the Nawa tehsil under Nagaur district, in the state of Rajasthan, India. It is one of 217 villages in nawa Block along with villages like Takiya, Thikariya and Naya Bas, Bawari dariya ka bas.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Talra Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The best time to visit Talra Wildlife Sanctuary is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Talra Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shimla Airport (Jubbarhatti) (68 km) and the nearest railway station is Dehradun Railway Station (82 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Talra Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Talra Wildlife Sanctuary at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Talra Wildlife Sanctuary?
faq.a: Stays in Talra Wildlife Sanctuary start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.
